results.description:
Click here!

Welcome to Todoist! We've created this guide to help you, as a team Admin, set up Todoist for your team or organization.

Let's start learning: this guide is a **project,** and it's broken up into **sections**, and each section has **tasks**.

A few things to know...

- The ⭐️ **Try it** section is tasks you should do to get Todoist set up for your team!
- Sections with a 📚 are learning sections. Here you'll find videos, articles, and product tours for more help if you need it.
- Check off your tasks as you finish them! ✅

Not sure where to start?

Folders: [Help guide](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/use-folders-to-organize-team-projects-uoElGQdbb) | [Video demo](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z4W6NkY8oU8)

Our recommendations below 👇

Folders should reflect how you organize or talk about your work. For example:

- If Todoist will be used by multiple teams or departments: create one folder per team/department
    - Example: Marketing, Engineering, Operations, Finance, Sales...
- If your work is client-based: create one folder per client
    - Example: Company A, Company B, Company C....
- If your team works on projects to achieve specific goals: create one folder per goal
    - Example: Make $10k in Q2, Double sign-ups, Achieve positive NPS score

Other ideas:

- A folder for 1:1s to manage manager-employee 1:1 meetings. Remember: **projects can be private!**
- A folder called "Onboarding" that houses onboarding projects and tasks for your new hires
Seed task for read/update testing (updated)
Projects: [Help Guide](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-projects-TLTjNftLM)

A few tips 👇

Sections: [Help guide](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-sections-rOrK0aEn) | [Video demo](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wkgvyEYfkIo)

Remember: Sections are great for adding more order to your project. It can also be helpful to think of the types of layout that will work best for your project.

A few recommendations -

- If your project has definitive end date:
    - Phases of completion (To Do, Doing, Done)
    - Hand-off points (In Product, In Design, In Dev, In Marketing)
- If your project is for a recurring meeting (like a 1:1):
    - Sections for: Agenda, Action Items

results.content: Experiment with layout and display options
results.description:
Some options 👇

List layout | [Help guide](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/use-the-list-layout-in-todoist-AMAhHMVRH)
Board layout | [Help guide](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/use-the-board-layout-in-todoist-AiAVsyEI)
Calendar layout | [Help guide](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/use-the-calendar-layout-in-todoist-lPHRQTu0o)

Need some help 👇

Introduction to tasks | [Help guide](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-tasks-080OAXric)

Here's how to do it!

Inviting teammates | [Help guide](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/invite-or-remove-people-from-your-todoist-team-fAfiZjRb)

results.content: Create filters to monitor your team's work
results.description:
Some [inspiration](https://www.todoist.com/inspiration/todoist-filters) ✨
Introduction to filters | [Help guide](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-filters-V98wIH)
You are 7x more likely to complete your tasks when you download Todoist on 2+ devices!

More help if you need it 👇

📝 [Use the inbox in Todoist](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/use-the-inbox-in-todoist-HwHvYErS)
📝 [Plan your day with the Today view](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/plan-your-day-with-the-today-view-UVUXaiSs)
📝 [Plan your week with the Upcoming view](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/plan-your-week-with-the-upcoming-view-OKOg1mR8)

headers.content-security-policy-report-only: default-src 'self' https://app.nango.dev https://api.nango.dev https://connect.nango.dev https://connect.nango.dev/;child-src 'self';connect-src 'self' https://*.google-analytics.com https://*.sentry.io https://app.nango.dev https://api.nango.dev wss://api.nango.dev/ https://connect.nango.dev https://connect.nango.dev/ https://*.posthog.com https://*.stripe.com https://*.plain.com wss://*.plain.com https://raw.githubusercontent.com;font-src 'self' data: https://*.googleapis.com https://*.gstatic.com https://*.cdn-plain.com;frame-src 'self' https://accounts.google.com https://app.nango.dev https://api.nango.dev https://connect.nango.dev https://connect.nango.dev/ https://www.youtube.com https://*.stripe.com;img-src 'self' data: blob: https://app.nango.dev https://api.nango.dev https://*.google-analytics.com https://*.googleapis.com https://*.posthog.com https://img.logo.dev https://*.ytimg.com https://*.plain.com;manifest-src 'self';media-src 'self';object-src 'self';script-src 'self' 'unsafe-eval' 'unsafe-inline' https://app.nango.dev https://api.nango.dev https://*.stripe.com https://*.google-analytics.com https://*.googleapis.com https://apis.google.com https://*.posthog.com https://www.youtube.com https://*.cdn-plain.com;style-src blob: 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://*.googleapis.com https://app.nango.dev https://api.nango.dev;worker-src blob: 'self' https://app.nango.dev https://api.nango.dev https://*.googleapis.com https://*.posthog.com;base-uri 'self';form-action 'self';frame-ancestors 'self';script-src-attr 'none';upgrade-insecure-requests
headers.access-control-expose-headers: Authorization, Etag, Content-Type, Content-Length, X-Nango-Signature, X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset
